skill.name: checkpoint
skill.description: Guide agents to save structured progress checkpoints to memory-keeper during long work sessions.
skill.summary: Save task state, modified files, progress, blockers, and next actions as recoverable checkpoints.
skill.author: Barnhardt-Enterprises-Inc

security_audit.summary: Static external-command findings are false positives caused by Markdown inline backticks and fenced examples, not shell execution. Static weak-crypto findings are false positives caused by text such as description labels, not cryptographic code. The remaining concern is medium privacy risk because the skill instructs agents to persist task state, modified file lists, blockers, and next actions to memory-keeper.

medium_findings.title: Persistent Storage of Session State
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill instructs agents to collect current task details, modified file lists, progress, blockers, and next actions, then save them to memory-keeper. This is legitimate checkpoint behavior, but it can persist sensitive project context if users do not redact secrets or confidential details.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly name the categories of session data to save and show memory-keeper save operations. The risk is privacy and retention, not confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Detected as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings occur in Markdown inline code, fenced examples, and output examples. No executable script, shell command, Ruby interpolation, or command injection path is present in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows these locations are Markdown formatting or illustrative memory-keeper calls. There is no shell syntax intended for execution.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Pattern from Plain Text
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The weak cryptography findings appear to match substrings in ordinary text such as description fields and table headers. No hashing, encryption, cipher selection, or cryptographic API usage is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain frontmatter, checkpoint text, or table labels rather than cryptographic code. No evidence of weak algorithm use was found.

content.value_statement: Long AI coding sessions can lose important context when work spans many steps. This skill defines a checkpoint routine for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code using memory-keeper.
actual_capabilities: Defines when to create progress checkpoints during long sessions.
Lists task state fields to capture, including task, files, blockers, and next action.
Provides memory-keeper save calls for progress keys and named checkpoints.
Includes a compaction preparation step for large context windows.
Gives checkpoint frequency guidance for implementation, testing, research, and debugging.
limitations: Requires a memory-keeper tool that supports context save and checkpoint operations.
Does not restore checkpoints itself; it references a separate recovery command.
Stores summaries and metadata, not full project snapshots or file contents.
May persist sensitive task details unless users redact them before saving.

use_cases.title: Protect Long Refactors
use_cases.description: Save task state after each major code change so work can resume after context loss.

use_cases.title: Track Debugging Progress
use_cases.description: Record tested hypotheses, blockers, and the next diagnostic step during complex debugging.

use_cases.title: Manage Research Sessions
use_cases.description: Capture research progress and next actions during multi-step technical investigation.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Checkpoint
prompt_templates.scenario: A beginner wants to save the current session state.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a checkpoint for my current task. Include the task, progress, modified files, blockers, and next action.

prompt_templates.title: Checkpoint Before Tests
prompt_templates.scenario: A user is about to run tests after making changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Save a checkpoint before I run tests. Note what changed, which tests I plan to run, and what result I expect.

prompt_templates.title: Debugging Checkpoint
prompt_templates.scenario: A user is testing several debugging hypotheses.
prompt_templates.prompt: Checkpoint this debugging session. Record the current hypothesis, evidence found, failed attempts, blockers, and the next diagnostic step.

prompt_templates.title: Compaction-Ready Checkpoint
prompt_templates.scenario: An advanced user needs a checkpoint before context compaction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a full checkpoint for possible context compaction. Save the current task, todo state, modified files, decisions, blockers, and exact restart step.

output_examples.input: Checkpoint this refactor before I continue.
output_examples.output: Checkpoint saved with the current refactor goal, changed file count, implementation phase, open blocker summary, and next action.
Progress saved and compaction preparation completed. The recovery note includes the task, files touched, current phase, and restart step.
Debugging checkpoint saved with the active hypothesis, tests already tried, current blocker, and the next diagnostic command to run.

best_practices: Checkpoint after each meaningful change, test run, or completed investigation step.
Keep next actions specific enough for another session to resume immediately.
Redact secrets, private customer data, and sensitive repository details before saving.
anti_patterns: Do not save raw secrets, credentials, tokens, or proprietary source content.
Do not rely on checkpoints as a replacement for version control.
Do not create vague checkpoints that omit the exact next action.

faq.question: What does this skill save?
faq.answer: It saves task summaries, modified file lists, progress, blockers, todo state, and next actions.

faq.question: Does it save full source files?
faq.answer: No. The skill describes summaries and metadata, not full source file snapshots.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Is this safe for private projects?
faq.answer: Use caution. Redact secrets and confidential details before saving checkpoints to persistent memory.

faq.question: Can it restore lost context?
faq.answer: It prepares data for recovery, but restoration depends on the separate recovery workflow.

faq.question: When should I create a checkpoint?
faq.answer: Create one after significant work, before large operations, before compaction, or when switching tasks.
